name: Metformin
mechanism: Reduces hepatic glucose production and improves insulin sensitivity
notes: First-line for most T2DM; avoid if eGFR < 30

name: Empagliflozin
mechanism: Blocks glucose reabsorption in kidney; promotes glucosuria
common_side_effects: UTI, genital mycotic infections, DKA risk
notes: Cardio- and renal-protective; avoid if eGFR < 20

name: Dapagliflozin
mechanism: Blocks glucose reabsorption in kidney; promotes glucosuria
notes: Proven kidney and heart failure benefit

name: Liraglutide
mechanism: Incretin mimetic; enhances insulin secretion and slows gastric emptying
notes: Weight loss benefit; cardiovascular protection; anti-inflammatory effects

name: Semaglutide
mechanism: Incretin mimetic; enhances insulin secretion and reduces appetite
notes: Superior A1c reduction and weight loss; weekly injection or daily oral

name: Insulin Glargine
mechanism: Long-acting insulin analogue providing 24h basal coverage
notes: Essential when beta cell function severely impaired

name: Insulin Aspart
mechanism: Rapid-acting insulin for mealtime glucose control
notes: Mealtime coverage; use with basal insulin

name: Pioglitazone
mechanism: PPARg agonist; improves insulin sensitivity in adipose and muscle
notes: Good for insulin resistance; avoid in heart failure

name: Linagliptin
mechanism: Prolongs endogenous incretin activity to enhance insulin secretion
notes: Well-tolerated; no renal adjustment needed; modest A1c reduction

name: Sitagliptin
mechanism: Prolongs endogenous incretin activity to enhance insulin secretion
notes: Good safety profile; reduce dose if eGFR < 45

name: Canagliflozin
common_side_effects: Genital infections, DKA risk, volume depletion

name: Dulaglutide
mechanism: Weekly incretin mimetic; beta cell support and appetite reduction

name: Pregabalin
mechanism: Calcium channel alpha-2-delta ligand; reduces neuropathic pain signaling
notes: First-line for diabetic peripheral neuropathy pain

name: Duloxetine
mechanism: Serotonin-norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor; central pain modulation

name: Atorvastatin
mechanism: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor; reduces LDL cholesterol
monitoring: Lipid panel, liver enzymes, CK if myalgia
common_side_effects: Myalgia, elevated liver enzymes, GI upset
notes: Recommended for most diabetic patients with CV risk

name: Ramipril
mechanism: Inhibits angiotensin-converting enzyme; renal and cardiac protection
notes: Renoprotective in diabetic nephropathy; first-line for HTN in DM
